name: Weird Facts
description: Surprising, counter-intuitive, and just plain odd trivia from across science and culture.

questions.q: Which is the only planet in our solar system that rotates clockwise (when viewed from above)?

source.notes: Uranus is technically tilted on its side, but Venus rotates retrograde.

questions.q: What is the technical name for the # symbol (used for hashtags)?

source.notes: Coined at Bell Labs in the 1960s. ('Pound,' 'hash,' and 'number sign' are also valid common names.)

questions.q: How many time zones does Russia span?
Which country has more pyramids than Egypt?

source.notes: Sudan has ~250 pyramids (Nubian/Kushite) vs. ~118 in Egypt.

questions.q: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
Which mammal famously sweats milk?

source.notes: Monotremes (egg-laying mammals) lack nipples; milk seeps from skin pores.

questions.q: Which is the only fruit with seeds on the outside?

source.notes: Each visible 'seed' is actually a separate fruit (achene).

questions.q: What unusual color do bananas glow under blacklight (UV)?

source.notes: Due to chlorophyll breakdown products in ripening peels.

questions.q: Approximately how many times does an average human heart beat per day?
Which animal can sleep with one eye open and half its brain awake?

source.notes: Called 'unihemispheric slow-wave sleep.' Allows them to surface-breathe and watch for predators.

questions.q: Which is the only continent that lies entirely in the Southern Hemisphere?

source.notes: Australia and South America cross the equator only barely; Africa straddles it.

questions.q: Which is the smallest U.S. state by area?
Which is the longest-living vertebrate, with some specimens over 400 years old?
Which sea creatures hold hands while sleeping so they don't drift apart?

source.notes: And often sleep wrapped in kelp for the same reason.

questions.q: Approximately how many miles of blood vessels does an adult human body contain?

source.notes: Enough to circle the Earth more than twice.

questions.q: What color of light is most strongly scattered in Earth's atmosphere, making the sky blue?
How long is the Pacific island Nauru's longest river?

source.notes: Nauru is one of the few countries with no rivers (Saudi Arabia and Vatican City are others).

questions.q: Which everyday item is the only thing that can scratch a diamond?

source.notes: Diamond is rated 10 on the Mohs scale.

questions.q: Which animal has fingerprints almost indistinguishable from human ones?

source.notes: Koala fingerprints have been mistaken for human prints at crime scenes.

questions.q: Approximately how long does it take for sunlight to travel from the Sun to Earth?
